project.name: Chunkline
project.goal: A Rust library that splits large text files into stable, overlap-aware chunks for indexing pipelines.
project.primary_user: A backend developer building document indexing who needs deterministic chunk boundaries.
project.core_problem: Ad-hoc chunkers produce different boundaries across runs and platforms, which invalidates cached embeddings and makes diffs noisy.
project.core_journey:
1. The developer adds the library and configures chunk size and overlap.
2. They feed it a text stream.
3. They receive deterministic chunks with stable ids that survive re-runs on unchanged input.
project.success_measure: Chunking the same 100MB corpus twice yields byte-identical chunk sets and ids on two different machines.
project.success_metrics:
- determinism -- byte-identical chunk ids across two runs and two platforms
- throughput -- at least 50MB/s on the reference corpus
project.riskiest_assumption: Deterministic boundary selection can stay fast enough (50MB/s) without unsafe code.
project.req_ac_list:
- [ ] **REQ-AC1:** Chunking identical input twice yields identical chunk ids and boundaries.
- [ ] **REQ-AC2:** Overlap configuration is honored exactly at every boundary.
- [ ] **REQ-AC3:** A file smaller than one chunk yields exactly one chunk with a stable id.
project.non_goals:
- No tokenizer-aware chunking in the first iteration; bytes and characters only.
- No async API; the library is synchronous.
project.constraint_time: Side project, one evening a week.
project.constraint_data: Test corpora from public-domain texts only.
project.deployment_target: Published as a crate on crates.io; consumed as a library.
project.scale_expectations: Library consumers processing files up to a few GB.
project.in_scope_list:
- Deterministic size/overlap chunking over byte and char boundaries.
- Stable chunk ids derived from content.
- A benchmark harness for the throughput target.
project.current_alternative: hand-rolled splitters that drift between runs
project.key_benefit: chunks that never move unless the text does
project.key_differentiator: determinism is the contract, verified by cross-platform tests
